summary: Revision v2 successfully resolves all 8 blockers from round 1 (test strategy, observability, migration mechanism, force semantics, polymorphic FK, prose_blocks design, deprecation separation, Loom decoupling). The 10 new blockers surfaced this round are finer-grained — they live at the implementation-detail layer rather than the design-coherence layer. Highest-signal items are convergent across reviewers: pack rename safety (3 reviewers), Wardline trust model (3 reviewers), Loom URI registry coordination (2 reviewers). Recommend a focused B1-B7 fix pass; B8-B10 can be inlined during implementation.

progress_vs_round_1.interpretation: The plan is now structurally sound. Remaining blockers are localizable fixes, not redesigns.

blocking_issues.title: Pack rename has multiple silent-breakage surfaces
blocking_issues.convergence: high — caught by 3 reviewers from different angles

evidence.systems: core.py lines 337/465/531 hardcode 'planning' as default enabled_packs; templates_data.py lines 823/1531 list requires_packs: ['core', 'planning'] for release and roadmap packs; templates.py:898 confirms requires_packs is NEVER enforced at runtime — failure is silent orphan-relation, not load error as deprecation §3 claims
evidence.architecture: cross-pack relationships in release/roadmap reference planning.milestone — schema implications for existing DBs with release pack loaded; do internal template references update automatically or need DB migration?
evidence.reality: release pack has undocumented second cross-pack relation (release_item_from_task) — inventory in deprecation §2.2 is incomplete; milestone count in deprecation §2.3/§8.1 says '2 milestones' but actual is 6 (3 archived, 3 completed) + 12 phases + 27 steps

blocking_issues.resolution: Phase 3 of deprecation plan must enumerate: (a) the three hardcoded sites in core.py with config-rewrite migration, (b) a step that rewrites 'planning'→'coordination' in every .filigree.conf, (c) requires_packs string updates in templates_data.py for release and roadmap packs, (d) correct the milestone count in §2.3 and §8.1 to reflect dogfood reality (6 milestones, 12 phases, 27 steps), (e) add release_item_from_task to the cross-pack inventory in §2.2, (f) add a DB-migration step (or proof of no-op) for existing release/roadmap-loaded DBs whose stored templates reference planning.*, (g) fix the false claim in §3 that pack-load fails loudly — document the actual silent-breakage failure mode.

blocking_issues.title: Batch 3 / deprecation circular dependency
blocking_issues.evidence: Shuttle §13 says Batch 3 prerequisite is filigree deprecation 'executing or executed'; Deprecation §6 says the plan runs *after* Shuttle Batch 3 (because migration targets must exist). Each waits on the other.
blocking_issues.resolution: Replace 'executing or executed' in Shuttle §13 with 'ratified' (or equivalent: design approved + Batch 3 acceptance criteria include migration-target readiness). Tighten Deprecation §6 sequencing language to remove circularity.
blocking_issues.blocked_on: Shuttle doc + Deprecation doc — coordinated edit

blocking_issues.title: loom-core extraction risk understated; Batch 0 naming contradiction
blocking_issues.evidence: Section 12 calls loom-core extraction 'prerequisite, not Batch 0'; Section 13 labels the same work 'Batch 0' — direct naming contradiction. 'Mechanical, ~2-3 weeks, low risk' understates: db_issues.py >1000 LOC wired into FiligreeDB MRO mixin chain; extraction requires defining a clean generic interface, not file moves.
blocking_issues.resolution: Pick one name (recommend: Batch 0). Revise estimate to acknowledge interface-design phase precedes mechanical extraction. Add explicit acceptance gate: 'filigree CI green throughout extraction' as exit criterion.

blocking_issues.title: Loom URI registry split-brain — undetectable and unresolved in multi-project case

evidence.architecture: URI spec §5.1 places registry at .loom/registry.json; Shuttle §3.1 places .shuttle/shuttle.db co-resident with project; one filigree process can serve multiple projects — which registry does Shuttle read at startup? Open Question 11.4 admits the multi-project case without resolving it.
evidence.systems: Flat-file registry is propagated manually; stale peer's base_url produces 'unavailable' indefinitely, indistinguishable from 'peer down.' No divergence detection.

blocking_issues.resolution: Two coordinated fixes: (1) resolve §11.4 — specify whether Shuttle picks per-project registry, a merged process-level view, or refuses multi-project deployment; (2) each component publishes registry hash via X-Loom-Registry-Version response header with caller-side counter-check and a registry_divergence_total metric. Both decisions belong in the URI spec; Shuttle references them.

blocking_issues.title: Canary 11.4 metric cannot measure what it claims
blocking_issues.evidence: shuttle_tdd_event_omission_ratio is described as detecting agents who skip red_committed; but §4.5 enforces ordering at write path — green_committed without prior red_committed is REFUSED with OUT_OF_ORDER_EVENT. The metric cannot observe what write-path enforcement prevents.
blocking_issues.resolution: Reframe canary 11.4: replace with a background query over closed TDD steps checking for incomplete event sequences (e.g., steps closed without red_committed at all, or with red but no green). The intent is achievable — it requires a different measurement strategy.

blocking_issues.title: Contract-test CI gate has no implementation mechanism
blocking_issues.evidence: 'Gated on Loom fixtures published' is not a runnable CI condition. No fixture repository named, no owner specified, no CI integration described (submodule, download step, artifact).
blocking_issues.resolution: Specify in URI spec §10.4: fixture repository name/location, who owns publication, CI consumption mechanism (git submodule? versioned artifact download? pinned commit SHA?). Shuttle §8 then references the specified mechanism rather than declaring an abstract gate.

blocking_issues.title: Pre/post-check strings accumulate unvalidated until Wardline ships
blocking_issues.evidence: Close validator checks field PRESENCE, not string format. With Wardline 6-12 months away, every stored pre_checks/post_checks string will need retroactive evaluation. No format migration path planned.
blocking_issues.resolution: Enforce a prefix convention from Batch 3 forward (e.g., 'wardline:', 'local:', 'external:'). Bounds the namespace before accumulation begins. Document the prefix table and reserve future expansion in the spec.

blocking_issues.title: Test layout convention mismatch in Shuttle §8
blocking_issues.evidence: Shuttle §8 claims tests/{unit,integration,contract} layout 'mirrors filigree's convention.' Filigree actually organizes tests by surface: tests/{api,cli,core,migrations,install}/. tests/unit/ in filigree contains only 2 files. False premise inherited by implementor.
blocking_issues.resolution: Restructure §8 to mirror actual filigree layout (tests/{api,cli,core,migrations,contract,...}) or honestly state 'Shuttle adopts a different test layout because X.' Pick one.

blocking_issues.title: Milestone count factually wrong in deprecation §2.3 and §8.1
blocking_issues.evidence: Deprecation doc claims '2 milestones' in dogfood DB; actual count is 6 milestones (3 archived, 3 completed) + 12 phases + 27 steps. Pre-deployment validation test (8.1) asserts 'All 2 live milestones survive.'
blocking_issues.resolution: Correct the count. Replace 'unknown — varies by project' for phase/step with actual dogfood numbers. Distinguish dogfood-specific assertions from cross-project parameterized ones.
blocking_issues.blocked_on: internal to deprecation doc — folded into B1 resolution

blocking_issues.title: OTel and Prometheus framed as filigree pattern but filigree uses neither
blocking_issues.evidence: Filigree does NOT use OpenTelemetry, has NO Prometheus /metrics endpoint. Shuttle's observability stack is new, not extracted. Claim 'matches filigree's pattern' overclaims.
blocking_issues.resolution: Reframe §11 honestly: 'Shuttle introduces OTel + Prometheus; filigree has neither today and may adopt them later via loom-core.' This is fine — just don't claim symmetry that does not exist.

warnings.title: Batch 1 mutable prose column is self-inflicted migration debt
warnings.recommendation: Ship prose_blocks from Batch 1 — eliminates the Batch 1→2 migration and the guaranteed authorship-history loss event.

warnings.title: work_package routing has three different answers
warnings.recommendation: §5.2 (route to Shuttle stage) vs §9.4 (re-evaluate as canary) vs §10.1 (--keep-work-packages opt-in) — specify the default and conditions for deviation.

warnings.title: HMAC v1 trusted-execution-environment assumption not stated
warnings.recommendation: Add explicit threat-model statement: 'v1 federation assumes all peers run in a mutually trusted execution environment; remote-process federation requires v2 token bundles.'

warnings.title: decision_reads table underspecified
warnings.recommendation: Add schema catalog entry, idempotency handling, index design, compaction question. The unread_decisions cap depends on these.

warnings.title: Migration manifest format unspecified
warnings.recommendation: Specify manifest schema; rollback consumes it but the format is never defined.

warnings.title: Step.estimated_minutes omitted from migration field-mapping table
warnings.recommendation: Add to deprecation §2.x — decide map vs drop.

warnings.title: Releases Loom router is currently an empty stub
warnings.recommendation: Either implement the router or flag 'release/release_item resolvable kinds: pending router implementation' in URI spec §registry.

warnings.title: loom-uri fixture directory presented as existing
warnings.recommendation: Mark docs/federation/loom-uri/fixtures/ as 'to be created' in URI spec §10.4.

warnings.title: record_event null-key is permissive for ordering-enforced events
warnings.recommendation: Make idempotency_key REQUIRED at the API surface for red_committed, green_committed, post_checks, ready_for_review — prevents duplicate events on SQLite BUSY retry.

warnings.title: STALE_SUPERSESSION retry has no bounded backoff
warnings.recommendation: Bounded retries with jitter; add shuttle_supersession_conflict_total metric to surface the reinforcing loop.

warnings.title: 7-day reversibility window uses time as proxy for the wrong invariant
warnings.recommendation: Replace with event-based predicate: manifest records migration_timestamp; rollback queries events since timestamp and refuses if any write events found on migrated objects.

warnings.title: Migration tool version drift against Shuttle MCP surface
warnings.recommendation: Tool version-checks Shuttle MCP on startup; pin supported range.

warnings.title: ready_for_review orphans wait 7 days to become visible
warnings.recommendation: Add clarion_last_seen heartbeat on dashboard from day one — operator sees Clarion-missing immediately, not after TTL.

warnings.title: Token leak has no v1 incident-response procedure
warnings.recommendation: Document env var to rotate, peers to restart, rotation-gap duration.

warnings.title: prose_blocks has no compaction path
warnings.recommendation: compact_events applies to the event log only; specify prose_blocks compaction or commit to unbounded growth.

warnings.title: Multi-project deployment topology unspecified
warnings.recommendation: Coordinated with B4 — define how Shuttle runs in single-process-many-projects deployments.

warnings.title: force_used alert has no threshold
warnings.recommendation: Specify the rate/count that triggers an alert.

warnings.title: HMAC has no anti-replay protection; not disclosed
warnings.recommendation: Either add nonce/timestamp window or document explicitly that v1 accepts replay.

warnings.title: PII redaction via supersession insufficient for GDPR
warnings.recommendation: Supersession leaves prior versions; document a hard-erase path or scope the system as PII-free.

warnings.title: Deprecation Phase 3 stakeholder communication is thin
warnings.recommendation: Add explicit comms plan to deprecation §3.

warnings.title: Migration partial-failure has no operator procedure
warnings.recommendation: Specify what an operator does when 80% of objects migrate and 20% fail.

warnings.title: Unregistered URI status has no cleanup guidance
warnings.recommendation: Specify TTL or operator action.

warnings.title: Rollback-after-window has no forward-fix path
warnings.recommendation: If window expires and rollback is needed, what's the procedure?

warnings.title: loom-core extraction has no acceptance gate
warnings.recommendation: Folded into B3 resolution.

warnings.title: Batch 3 go/no-go has no named coordinator
warnings.recommendation: Name a decision owner.

warnings.title: __ prefix reservation is test spec not implementation spec
warnings.recommendation: Specify enforcement point (DB constraint? API validator?).

high_signal_convergent_issues.note: Three reviewers caught variations: Reality (inventory gaps), Architecture (cross-pack schema implications), Systems (hardcoded sites + silent failure mode). The deprecation plan §3's failure-mode claim is empirically wrong — Systems verified at templates.py:898.
Wardline named-only (Reality), HMAC trust assumption (Architecture), pre/post-string accumulation (Systems). Three angles on the same future-coupling risk.
Architecture caught the multi-project coordination directly; Systems caught registry split-brain detection. Both belong in URI spec.

reality: Releases Loom router is empty stub (W7)
architecture: loom-core risk understated + Batch 0 naming contradiction (B3)
quality: Contract-test CI gate has no implementation mechanism (B6)
systems: requires_packs never enforced at runtime — silent breakage (B1 evidence)

blocked_on_external_work: Wardline-related warnings (W3, B7) cannot be fully resolved until Wardline exists; mitigations specifiable now

single_most_actionable_next_step: Resolve B1 (pack rename safety). Three reviewers converged on it; the empirical failure mode contradicts the deprecation plan's stated assumption; the fix is bounded and internal to one document. After B1, address B2 (circular dependency — one-line edit) and B4 (registry coordination — requires URI spec edit).

reality.note: Most factual checks passed — loom-core primitives exist, pack types verified, federation reality vs aspiration accurate

architecture.note: Pattern alignment strong; batch tracer-bullet structure sound; issues localized to specific contradictions and underspecification

quality.note: Test/observability foundation present (round 1 fix held); finer-grained gaps in measurability and CI mechanism

systems.note: Empirical verification of failure modes is this reviewer's signature contribution; B1 silent-failure proof at templates.py:898 is the single highest-value finding of the round
